title: Enterprise: Allow different item viewing permission based on Board Roles (Get rid of "Item viewing limitations will apply to all roles except owners)
excerpt:
Description
Allow different item viewing permissions based on board role. Currently, it’s all or nothing: “Item viewing limitations will apply to all roles except owners”. 
What are you trying to achieve
Allows for team …

posts.author: UFAI (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Allow different item viewing permissions based on board role. Currently, it’s all or nothing: “Item viewing limitations will apply to all roles except owners”.

What are you trying to achieve

Allows for team members with the roles such as “Contributor” and “Editor” to see all items in a board, but restricts other roles such as “Viewer” to only see only items they are assigned.

Right now, all of my team members HAVE to be board owners if we want to restrict “Viewers” to only see assigned items. But because team members are board owners they can do things I don’t want them to do, and can potentially screw the entire board up (even just on accident).

By allowing item viewing permissions on a role basis, I could set my team members to “Contributors” so they can see all items and can only edit things within certain columns, the rest of my organization are default “Viewers” and can post updates on their items, and the Admins get to be board owners.

posts.author: ShaniAb (shani)
posts.cookedHtml:
UFAI:

Allow different item viewing permissions based on board role. Currently, it’s all or nothing: “Item viewing limitations will apply to all roles except owners”.

What are you trying to achieve

Allows for team members with the roles such as “Contributor” and “Editor” to see all items in a board, but restricts other roles such as “Viewer” to only see only items they are assigned.

Right now, all of my team members HAVE to be board owners if we want to restrict “Viewers” to only see assigned items. But because team members are board owners they can do things I don’t want them to do, and can potentially screw the entire board up (even just on accident).

By allowing item viewing permissions on a role basis, I could set my team members to “Contributors” so they can see all items and can only edit things within certain columns, the rest of my organization are default “Viewers” and can post updates on their items, and the Admins get to be board owners.

I completely agree with this request and have encountered the same limitation.

In my organization, there is a real need to segregate sensitive information between different user roles, while still allowing those users to actively update and collaborate on project tasks.

Managing this through separate boards is not a viable solution for us, especially when working with a single project structure (including timelines and Gantt views). Without role-based item viewing permissions, users are exposed to information they should not have access to, which creates both security and governance challenges.

This feature would make it possible to maintain proper access control without forcing team members into board owner roles or duplicating project boards.
